prophecies.q: 朕之天下，今稍定矣。卿深明易道，不知何人始丧我国家？以及我朝之后，登极者何人？得传者何代？卿为朕历历言之。
prophecies.a:
欲知将来，当观已往。得贤者治，失贤者丧，此万世不易之道也。

白话文翻译：想要知道未来的事，应当先看看过去。得到贤才的国家就能治理好，失去贤才的国家就会灭亡，这是万古不变的道理。
prophecies.q_en: My realm is now somewhat settled. You are well-versed in the Way of Change — who shall be the first to bring ruin to my dynasty? After my reign, who shall ascend the throne? Which dynasties shall succeed? Tell me in detail.
prophecies.a_en:
To know the future, one must look to the past. Those who gain the worthy shall govern well; those who lose the worthy shall perish. This is the unchanging principle of all ages.

Vernacular: To foresee the future, one should first examine the past. A nation that obtains wise men will prosper; one that loses them will fall. This is an eternal and unchanging truth.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此为藏头诗开篇总论，李淳风以「得贤者治，失贤者丧」概括治乱之道。纵观中国历代兴亡，确实与用人得失密切相关。
prophecies.verdict_en: This is the opening statement of Cang Tou Shi. Li Chunfeng summarizes the principle of governance: those who gain the worthy prosper, those who lose them perish. Throughout Chinese dynastic history, the rise and fall of states has indeed been closely tied to the employment of talent.

prophecies.q: 朕所问者，非此之谓也。欲卿以术数之学，推我朝得享几许年？至何人乱我国家？
prophecies.a:
乱我朝之天下者，即在君侧。三十年后，杀唐之子孙殆尽，主自不知耳。

白话文翻译：颠覆大唐天下的人，就在陛下身边。三十年后，她将杀尽唐朝的子孙后代，而陛下如今还不知道。
prophecies.q_en: That is not what I am asking. I want you to use the art of divination to calculate how many years my dynasty shall endure, and who shall bring chaos to my realm.
prophecies.a_en:
The one who shall bring chaos to your dynasty is already at your side. Thirty years hence, she will slaughter the descendants of Tang nearly to extinction — yet Your Majesty does not know it.

Vernacular: The person who will overthrow the Tang is right beside you now. In thirty years, she will kill nearly all of the Tang royal descendants, but you are currently unaware.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 武则天当时为唐太宗才人，贞观年间即在「君侧」。太宗驾崩（649年）约三十年后的690年，武则天废唐睿宗、改国号为周，大肆屠杀李唐宗室，与预言完全吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: Wu Zetian was a concubine of Emperor Taizong at the time, literally 'at the emperor's side.' Approximately thirty years after Taizong's death (649), in 690, Wu Zetian deposed Emperor Ruizong, renamed the dynasty Zhou, and massacred the Li-Tang royal family — matching the prophecy exactly.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.q: 此人何姓？何名？卿为朕明言之。
prophecies.a:
天机不可泄，泄之有殃。臣但以天象告之。其为人也，止戈不离身，两目长在空。实如斯也。

白话文翻译：天机不可泄露，泄露会招来灾祸。臣只能以天象暗示：此人的特征是——「止戈」合为「武」字，「两目在空」合为「曌」字。确实如此。
prophecies.q_en: What is this person's surname? What is their name? Tell me plainly.
prophecies.a_en:
Heaven's secrets must not be revealed — to reveal them invites calamity. I can only speak through celestial signs. This person's nature: 'the weapon never leaves the body' and 'two eyes forever in the sky.' It is truly so.

Vernacular: Heaven's secrets cannot be disclosed, for disclosure brings disaster. I can only hint through astronomical signs: 'weapon never leaving the body' combines to form the character 'Wu' (武), and 'two eyes in the sky' forms the character 'Zhao' (曌). This is indeed the case.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「止戈」合为「武」字，即武则天之姓。「两目长在空」合为「曌」（zhào），是武则天登基后自创之名字，意为日月当空。字谜与史实完全吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: The characters for 'stop' (止) and 'spear' (戈) combine to form 'Wu' (武), Wu Zetian's surname. 'Two eyes in the sky' forms 'Zhao' (曌), the name Wu Zetian created for herself after ascending the throne, meaning sun and moon shining overhead. The character puzzle matches historical fact exactly.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.q: 此人灭唐之后，又有何人出来治世？
prophecies.a:
有文曲星下界，生于卖豆腐之家，后来为相，自能平之。此人之后，又有五十年太平气象。

白话文翻译：有文曲星降世，出生于卖豆腐的人家，后来做了宰相，自然能够平定局面。此人之后，又有五十年的太平景象。
prophecies.q_en: After this person destroys the Tang, who shall emerge to restore order?
prophecies.a_en:
The Star of Literary Brilliance shall descend to earth, born into a family of tofu sellers. He shall later become prime minister and naturally restore order. After him, there shall be fifty years of peace.

Vernacular: A star of scholarly talent will descend to the mortal world, born into a tofu merchant's family. He will later become prime minister and restore stability. After him, there will be fifty years of peace.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「文曲星」暗指狄仁杰，他推荐张柬之等五人发动神龙政变（705年），推翻武周、复辟唐朝。「生于卖豆腐之家」虽无确切史料佐证，但民间传说狄仁杰出身寒门。复唐后的开元盛世确有数十年太平。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'Star of Literary Brilliance' alludes to Di Renjie, who recommended Zhang Jianzhi and four others to launch the Shenlong Coup (705), overthrowing the Zhou dynasty and restoring Tang rule. 'Born into a tofu seller's family' lacks definitive historical evidence, but folk tradition holds Di Renjie came from humble origins. The Kaiyuan era of prosperity that followed did bring decades of peace.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.q: 五十年太平之后又如何？
prophecies.a:
前二十四年可媲美于尧舜，后二十四年又有乱天下者。危而不危，一人大口，逢杨而生，遇郭而止。

白话文翻译：前二十四年可与尧舜盛世媲美，后二十四年又出现搅乱天下的人。看似危险却不至灭亡——「一人大口」合为「安」字，因杨贵妃而起事，因郭子仪而平定。
prophecies.q_en: What happens after the fifty years of peace?
prophecies.a_en:
The first twenty-four years shall rival the reigns of Yao and Shun. The latter twenty-four years shall see another who brings chaos to the realm. Perilous yet not fatal — 'one person, large mouth'; born from Yang, ended by Guo.

Vernacular: The first twenty-four years will be comparable to the golden ages of Yao and Shun. The latter twenty-four years will see a new troublemaker. The situation seems dangerous but the dynasty survives — 'one person with a large mouth' forms the character 'An' (安); the rebellion arises because of Lady Yang and is quelled by General Guo.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 唐玄宗开元年间（713-741）确为盛世，约二十四年。天宝年间安禄山起兵叛乱（755年）。「一人大口」合为「安」字，即安禄山。「逢杨而生」指因杨贵妃受宠导致杨国忠专权而激发叛乱，「遇郭而止」指郭子仪平定安史之乱。与史实高度吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: The Kaiyuan era of Emperor Xuanzong (713-741) was indeed a golden age of roughly twenty-four years. The An Lushan Rebellion broke out in 755. 'One person, large mouth' combines to form 'An' (安), i.e., An Lushan. 'Born from Yang' refers to Yang Guifei's favor leading to Yang Guozhong's corrupt power, which provoked the rebellion. 'Ended by Guo' refers to General Guo Ziyi who quelled the An-Shi Rebellion. Highly consistent with historical fact.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
越五十年，稍稍太平。后六十年，混世魔王下界，日月生于面目，杀人无数，血流成河。幸有独眼龙平治之。后又树挂拐尺者乱之。此时天下荒乱，人民饥饿，四十年中有五火猪更递为君。

白话文翻译：过了五十年，天下稍稍太平。再过六十年，有混世魔王降临人间，名字中有日月二字，杀人无数、血流成河。幸亏有「独眼龙」出来平定。之后又有「树挂拐尺」之人作乱。此后天下荒乱、人民饥饿，四十年间有五个如火如荼的诸侯轮流称帝。
prophecies.q_en: What happens after the An-Shi Rebellion?
prophecies.a_en:
After fifty more years, there will be some peace. Then after sixty years, a Demon King descends — sun and moon born upon his face, killing countless people, rivers of blood. Fortunately, 'One-Eyed Dragon' shall pacify him. Then one who 'hangs a carpenter's square on a tree' causes further chaos. The realm falls into famine and disorder; within forty years, five fiery rulers take turns on the throne.

Vernacular: After fifty years of relative peace, sixty years later a great tyrant appears. His name contains the characters for sun and moon; he kills countless people and causes rivers of blood. Fortunately, the 'One-Eyed Dragon' emerges to restore order. Afterward, another rebel causes chaos. The land falls into famine and disorder, with five warlords taking turns as emperor over forty years.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「混世魔王」指唐末黄巢起义（875-884），「日月生于面目」可解为黄巢之「黄」字。「独眼龙」指李克用，沙陀族将领，以一目失明著称，助唐平定黄巢。「五火猪更递为君」指五代时期（907-960），后梁、后唐、后晋、后汉、后周五个短命王朝更替。与唐末五代史实基本吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'Demon King' refers to Huang Chao's rebellion (875-884). 'One-Eyed Dragon' refers to Li Keyong, a Shatuo general known for having lost one eye, who helped suppress the Huang Chao revolt. 'Five fiery rulers taking turns' refers to the Five Dynasties period (907-960) — Later Liang, Later Tang, Later Jin, Later Han, and Later Zhou. Largely consistent with late Tang and Five Dynasties history.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.q: 五代之后又有何人治世？
prophecies.a:
有真龙降世，走随小月，阳火应运，木时戴帽，开天地之文运，启斯世之朦胧。礼乐作，教化兴，真太平有道之世也。

白话文翻译：有真龙降临人世，「走随小月」合为「赵」字，「阳火应运」指丙火即宋，「木时戴帽」为「宋」字。此人开启天地的文运，拨开蒙昧的迷雾。礼乐兴盛，教化大行，是真正太平有道的盛世。
prophecies.q_en: After the Five Dynasties, who shall bring order?
prophecies.a_en:
A true dragon shall descend to earth. 'Walking alongside a small moon' — 'yang fire responds to destiny' — 'wood wearing a hat.' He shall open the literary destiny of heaven and earth, dispelling the darkness of the age. Rites and music shall flourish, education shall prosper — a truly peaceful and righteous era.

Vernacular: A true dragon descends to the mortal world. 'Walking alongside a small moon' combines to form the character 'Zhao' (赵); 'yang fire responds to destiny' indicates the Song dynasty; 'wood wearing a hat' forms the character 'Song' (宋). This ruler opens a new era of literary flourishing and dispels ignorance. Rites and music prosper, education thrives — a truly peaceful and enlightened age.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「走随小月」合为「赵」字，指赵匡胤。「木时戴帽」为「宋」字。赵匡胤于960年建立宋朝，结束五代乱世。宋朝以文治著称，文化、科技、教育空前繁荣，确为「礼乐作、教化兴」的时代，与预言高度吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Walking alongside a small moon' forms the character 'Zhao' (赵), referring to Zhao Kuangyin. 'Wood wearing a hat' forms 'Song' (宋). Zhao Kuangyin established the Song dynasty in 960, ending the chaos of the Five Dynasties. The Song dynasty was renowned for its cultural achievement, with unprecedented flourishing in culture, science, and education — truly an era of 'rites, music, and education,' highly consistent with the prophecy.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
有一兀之主兴焉，人皆披发，头生花，听其语不知其音，视其人恶见其面，乃胡人之主也。其国祚短，不过百年。

白话文翻译：有一个「兀」字当头的主人兴起，人们都披散着头发，头上插花。听他们说话听不懂声音，看他们的面孔令人惊异——这是胡人的君主。其国运短暂，不超过百年。
prophecies.q_en: How long shall Song's peace endure?
prophecies.a_en:
A ruler with 'wu' (兀) at the head shall arise. The people all wear their hair loose with flowers on their heads. Hearing their speech, one cannot understand their words; seeing their faces, one recoils — this is a ruler of the barbarians. His dynasty shall be short, not exceeding a hundred years.

Vernacular: A ruler whose name begins with the character 'wu' (兀) shall rise to power. The people all wear their hair unbound with flowers in their hair. Their speech is unintelligible, their appearance foreign — this is a barbarian emperor. His dynasty will be brief, lasting no more than a hundred years.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「一兀」合为「元」字，指蒙古忽必烈建立元朝（1271年）。「人皆披发、头生花」描述蒙古人发式和装饰习俗。「听其语不知其音」指蒙古语与汉语不通。元朝国祚98年（1271-1368），确实不足百年，与预言吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'One-wu' (一兀) combines to form 'Yuan' (元), referring to Kublai Khan's establishment of the Yuan dynasty (1271). 'Hair loose with flowers' describes Mongol hairstyles and customs. 'Speech unintelligible' refers to the Mongol language barrier. The Yuan dynasty lasted 98 years (1271-1368), indeed under a hundred years, matching the prophecy.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
若非天生一牛，日月并行，天下几无人类也。女生须，男生子，地裂山崩矣。

白话文翻译：若不是天降一位属牛之人，让「日」「月」并行合为「明」字，天下几乎没有活人了。那时女人长出胡须、男人能怀子，天翻地覆、山崩地裂。
prophecies.q_en: Who shall gain the realm after the Yuan?
prophecies.a_en:
Were it not for a heaven-sent ox, with sun and moon marching side by side, there would be scarcely any people left. Women grow beards, men bear children — the earth splits and mountains crumble.

Vernacular: If not for a heaven-sent person born in the year of the Ox, with 'sun' and 'moon' combined to form 'Ming' (明), the land would be nearly devoid of people. Women growing beards and men bearing children signify a world turned upside down — earth splitting and mountains crumbling.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「日月并行」合为「明」字，指明朝。「天生一牛」指朱元璋，他出身放牛娃，属牛（存疑，朱元璋实属龙，但民间传说多称其放牛出身）。朱元璋于1368年建立明朝，推翻元朝暴政。「天下几无人类」描述元末战乱、瘟疫导致人口锐减的惨状。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Sun and moon marching together' combines to form 'Ming' (明), the Ming dynasty. 'Heaven-sent ox' refers to Zhu Yuanzhang, who was famously a cowherd in his youth. Zhu established the Ming dynasty in 1368, overthrowing Yuan tyranny. 'Scarcely any people left' describes the devastating population loss from late Yuan warfare and plague.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
有八旗常在身之主出焉。人皆口内生火，手上走马，头上生花，衣皆两截，天下几非人类矣。越二百余年。

白话文翻译：会出现一位以「八旗」为标志的君主。人们口中喷火（吸烟），手上走马（钟表指针），头上生花（帽子上的装饰），衣服分为两截（上衣下裤的满族服饰）。天下几乎不像人类的世界了。此朝延续二百余年。
prophecies.a_en:
A ruler bearing the Eight Banners shall emerge. People will have fire in their mouths, horses running on their hands, flowers on their heads, and clothing in two halves — the world shall hardly seem human. This shall last over two hundred years.

Vernacular: A ruler identified by the 'Eight Banners' will appear. People will have fire in their mouths (smoking tobacco), horses running on their hands (pocket watches), flowers growing from their heads (hat ornaments), and clothes cut in two pieces (Manchu-style garments). The world will seem utterly transformed. This dynasty lasts over two hundred years.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「八旗」明确指清朝满洲八旗制度，1644年清军入关建立清朝。「口内生火」指吸烟习俗传入中国，「手上走马」指西洋钟表传入，「头上生花」指满族帽饰翎羽，「衣皆两截」指满族上衣下裤取代汉族长袍。清朝国祚268年（1644-1912），确实「二百余年」。预言与史实高度吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'Eight Banners' clearly refers to the Manchu banner system of the Qing dynasty, which entered China in 1644. 'Fire in mouths' refers to tobacco smoking spreading to China. 'Horses on hands' refers to Western pocket watches. 'Flowers on heads' refers to Manchu hat plumes. 'Clothing in two halves' refers to the Manchu jacket-and-trousers replacing Chinese robes. The Qing lasted 268 years (1644-1912), indeed 'over two hundred years.' The prophecy is highly consistent with history.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
又有混世魔王出焉，头上生黄毛，目中长流水。太悲之世也。

白话文翻译：又有混世魔王降临人间，头上长着黄色的毛发，眼中常流眼泪。这是一个极其悲惨的时代。
prophecies.a_en:
Another Demon King shall arise, with yellow hair atop the head and tears ever flowing from the eyes. A most sorrowful age.

Vernacular: Another great tyrant will appear, with yellow hair on the head and eyes constantly streaming with tears. This will be an extremely tragic era.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「头上生黄毛」被解读为太平天国运动（1851-1864），太平军因信奉拜上帝教蓄发不剃、头裹黄巾。洪秀全自称天王，掀起大规模内战，导致数千万人死亡，确为「太悲之世」。但「混世魔王」的指代存在多种解读，也有学者认为指代清末列强入侵。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Yellow hair on the head' is interpreted as referring to the Taiping Rebellion (1851-1864), whose soldiers grew their hair out and wore yellow headbands as followers of a Christian-influenced faith. Hong Xiuquan declared himself Heavenly King, sparking a massive civil war that killed tens of millions — truly 'a most sorrowful age.' However, the identity of the 'Demon King' has multiple interpretations; some scholars suggest it refers to Western imperial invasions of late Qing China.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据藏头诗的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Cangtou Poem corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
幸有小天罡下界，扫除海内妖氛，人皆享福，不过八十年。

白话文翻译：幸好有小天罡星降临人世，扫除天下的妖魔邪气，人们都享受太平之福。但是好景不过八十年。
prophecies.a_en:
Fortunately, a Minor Celestial Marshal shall descend, sweeping away the demons across the land. All people shall enjoy blessings — but for no more than eighty years.

Vernacular: Fortunately, a minor heavenly general will descend to earth, clearing away the evil forces throughout the land. The people will all enjoy peace and happiness, but this good fortune will last no more than eighty years.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「小天罡下界」的指代存在争议，有人解读为曾国藩平定太平天国，也有人解读为后来的历史人物。「不过八十年」的起止时间也有多种说法。由于藏头诗此段之后的内容涉及近现代，解读分歧极大，学术界尚无定论。
prophecies.verdict_en: The identity of the 'Minor Celestial Marshal' is debated — some interpret it as Zeng Guofan who suppressed the Taiping Rebellion, others associate it with later historical figures. The 'eighty years' timeframe is also disputed. Since this and subsequent passages touch on modern history, interpretations diverge widely and no scholarly consensus exists.

prophecies.a:
后有九十年大乱，真主出焉。又有木葡之人出，赖文武二曲星扶之。如是者五十年。

白话文翻译：之后有九十年的大乱时期，真命天子出现。又有姓氏与「木葡」相关的人出现，依靠文武两位曲星辅佐他。如此太平约五十年。
prophecies.q_en: What happens after the eighty years?
prophecies.a_en:
Then follows ninety years of great upheaval, and a true sovereign shall emerge. Then one whose name relates to 'wood-grape' shall appear, supported by two stars of literature and martial arts. This shall last fifty years.

Vernacular: After that comes ninety years of great chaos, and a destined ruler emerges. Then a person associated with 'wood-grape' appears, relying on two talented ministers of civil and military affairs. This period of stability lasts about fifty years.

prophecies.a:
此后天下太平矣。华夷同，日月明，处处有善人。天地清宁，人心归正。此千年不易之象也。

白话文翻译：此后天下太平了。华夏与四方一统，日月光明，处处都有善良的人。天地清明安宁，人心归于正道。这是千年不变的太平景象。
prophecies.q_en: Shall the realm find lasting peace after this?
prophecies.a_en:
Thereafter the realm shall be at peace. Chinese and foreign peoples united, sun and moon shining bright, good people everywhere. Heaven and earth shall be clear and tranquil, the human heart returning to righteousness. This is the unchanging vision of a thousand years.

Vernacular: After this, the world will be at peace. All peoples will be united, the sun and moon will shine brightly, and good people will be found everywhere. Heaven and earth will be clear and peaceful, and the human heart will return to the righteous path. This is the enduring vision of a thousand years of peace.
